items.title: With Warren Buffett Done as CEO, Just 3 Stocks Make Up Almost 50% of Berkshire Hathaway
items.description: American Express, Apple, and Bank of America make up almost 50% of Berkshire Hathaway’s total holdings. Will that continue now that Warren Buffett has stepped down?

items.title: How Mastercard Is Diversifying Growth Beyond Card-Based Payments
items.description: MA is expanding beyond card payments as value-added services like AI-driven security and data solutions fuel revenue growth.

items.title: Why These 3 Mega-Caps Could Still Surprise Investors in 2026
items.description: Three durable compounders for 2026: Visa’s payment scale, Walmart’s 52-year dividend streak, and Amazon’s cloud-led rebound after a heavy AI spending cycle.

items.title: Barclays Maintains Equal-Weight on American Express, Raises Price Target to $367
items.description: Barclays  analyst Terry Ma   maintains American Express (NYSE:AXP) with a Equal-Weight and raises the price target from $355 to $367.

items.title: Here's How Much $100 Invested In American Express 15 Years Ago Would Be Worth Today
